abstract: Scikit-learn is a Python module integrating wide range of state-of-the-art machine learning algorithms for medium-scale supervised and unsupervised problems. abstract: The goal of precipitation nowcasting is to predict the future rainfall intensity in a local region over relatively short period time. In paper, we formulate as spatiotemporal sequence which both input prediction target are sequences. By extending fully connected LSTM (FC-LSTM) convolutional structures input-to-state state-to-state transitions, propose (ConvLSTM) use it build an end-to-end trainable model for problem. Experiments show that our ConvLSTM network captures correlations better consistently outperforms FC-LSTM state-of-the-art operational ROVER algorithm nowcasting.

title: Dropout as a Bayesian Approximation: Representing Model Uncertainty in\n Deep Learning

gives us uncertainty NNs -- extracting information

title: Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks: A Deep Learning Framework for Traffic Forecasting
abstract: Timely accurate traffic forecast is crucial for urban control and guidance. In this paper, we propose a novel deep learning framework, Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (STGCN), tackle time series problem in domain. Instead applying regular convolutional recurrent units, formulate on graphs build model with complete structures, which enable much faster training speed fewer parameters. Experiments show that our STGCN effectively captures comprehensive spatio-temporal correlations through modeling multi-scale networks consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines various real-world datasets.

We prove it is PAC, achieving near optimal performance except for Õ(SA) timesteps using O(SA) space, improving on the Õ(S2 A) bounds of best previous algorithms. This result proves efficient reinforcement learning possible without model MDP from experience. Learning takes place single continuous thread experience---no resets nor parallel sampling used. Beyond its smaller storage experience requirements, Delayed Q-learning's per-experience computation cost much less than that PAC

title: Long short-term memory recurrent neural network architectures for large scale acoustic modeling
abstract: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) is a specific recurrent neural network (RNN) architecture that was designed to model temporal sequences and their long-range dependencies more accurately than conventional RNNs. We recently showed RNNs are effective DNNs modeling, considering moderately-sized models trained on single machine. Here, introduce the first distributed training of using asynchronous stochastic gradient descent optimization cluster machines. show two-layer deep where each layer has linear projection can exceed state-of-the-art recognition performance. title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Medical Image Analysis: Full Training or Fine Tuning?
abstract: Training a deep convolutional neural network (CNN) from scratch is difficult because it requires large amount of labeled training data and great deal expertise to ensure proper convergence. A promising alternative fine-tune CNN that has been pre-trained using, for instance, set natural images. However, the substantial differences between medical images may advise against such knowledge transfer. In this paper, we seek answer following central question in context image analysis: Can use CNNs with sufficient fine-tuning eliminate need scratch? To address question, considered four distinct imaging applications three specialties (radiology, cardiology, gastroenterology) involving classification, detection, segmentation different modalities, investigated how performance trained compared fine-tuned layer-wise manner. Our experiments consistently demonstrated 1) adequate outperformed or, worst case, performed as well scratch; 2) were more robust size sets than 3) neither shallow tuning nor was optimal choice particular application; 4) our scheme could offer practical way reach best application at hand based on available data.

title: Quantized Neural Networks: Training Neural Networks with Low Precision Weights and Activations
abstract: We introduce a method to train Quantized Neural Networks (QNNs) --- neural networks with extremely low precision (e.g., 1-bit) weights and activations, at run-time. At train-time the quantized activations are used for computing parameter gradients. During forward pass, QNNs drastically reduce memory size accesses, replace most arithmetic operations bit-wise operations. As result, power consumption is expected be reduced. trained over MNIST, CIFAR-10, SVHN ImageNet datasets. The resulting achieve prediction accuracy comparable their 32-bit counterparts. For example, our version of AlexNet 1-bit 2-bit achieves $51\%$ top-1 accuracy. Moreover, we quantize gradients 6-bits as well which enables computation using only operation. recurrent were tested Penn Treebank dataset, achieved counterparts 4-bits. title: Deep Convolutional and LSTM Recurrent Neural Networks for Multimodal Wearable Activity Recognition
abstract: Human activity recognition (HAR) tasks have traditionally been solved using engineered features obtained by heuristic processes. Current research suggests that deep convolutional neural networks are suited to automate feature extraction from raw sensor inputs. However, human activities made of complex sequences motor movements, and capturing this temporal dynamics is fundamental for successful HAR. Based on the recent success recurrent time series domains, we propose a generic framework based LSTM units, which: (i) suitable multimodal wearable sensors; (ii) can perform fusion naturally; (iii) does not require expert knowledge in designing features; (iv) explicitly models activations. We evaluate our two datasets, one which has used public challenge. Our results show outperforms competing non-recurrent challenge dataset 4% average; outperforming some previous reported up 9%. be applied homogeneous modalities, but also fuse sensors improve performance. characterise key architectural hyperparameters’ influence performance provide insights about their optimisation.

title: Brain Tumor Segmentation Using Convolutional Neural Networks in MRI Images
abstract: Among brain tumors, gliomas are the most common and aggressive, leading to a very short life expectancy in their highest grade. Thus, treatment planning is key stage improve quality of oncological patients. Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) widely used technique assess these but large amount data produced by MRI prevents manual segmentation reasonable time, limiting use precise quantitative measurements clinical practice. So, automatic reliable methods required; however, spatial structural variability among tumors make challenging problem. In this paper, we propose an method based on Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), exploring small 3 ×3 kernels. The kernels allows designing deeper architecture, besides having positive effect against overfitting, given fewer number weights network. We also investigated intensity normalization as pre-processing step, which though not CNN-based methods, proved together with augmentation be effective for tumor images. Our proposal was validated Brain Tumor Segmentation Challenge 2013 database (BRATS 2013), obtaining simultaneously first position complete, core, enhancing regions Dice Similarity Coefficient metric (0.88, 0.83, 0.77) set. title: Understanding Neural Networks Through Deep Visualization
abstract: Recent years have produced great advances in training large, deep neural networks (DNNs), including notable successes convolutional (convnets) to recognize natural images. However, our understanding of how these models work, especially what computations they perform at intermediate layers, has lagged behind. Progress the field will be further accelerated by development better tools for visualizing and interpreting nets. We introduce two such here. The first is a tool that visualizes activations on each layer trained convnet as it processes an image or video (e.g. live webcam stream). found looking change response user input helps build valuable intuitions about convnets work. second enables features DNN via regularized optimization space. Because previous versions this idea less recognizable images, here we several new regularization methods combine produce qualitatively clearer, more interpretable visualizations. Both are open source work pre-trained with minimal setup.

title: Vision-Controlled Micro Flying Robots: From System Design to Autonomous Navigation and Mapping in GPS-Denied Environments
abstract: Autonomous microhelicopters will soon play a major role in tasks like search and rescue, environment monitoring, security surveillance, inspection. If they are further realized small scale, can also be used narrow outdoor indoor environments represent only limited risk for people. However, such operations, navigating based on global positioning system (GPS) information is not sufficient. Fully autonomous operation cities or other dense requires to fly at low altitudes, where GPS signals often shadowed, indoors actively explore unknown while avoiding collisions creating maps. This involves number of challenges all levels helicopter design, perception, actuation, control, navigation, which still have solved. The Swarm Micro Flying Robots (SFLY) project was European Union-funded with the goal swarm vision-controlled microaerial vehicles (MAVs) capable three-dimensional (3-D) mapping, optimal surveillance coverage GPS-denied environments. title: Valkyrie: NASA's First Bipedal Humanoid Robot
abstract: In December 2013, 16 teams from around the world gathered at Homestead Speedway near Miami, FL to participate in DARPA Robotics Challenge (DRC) Trials, an aggressive robotics competition partly inspired by aftermath of Fukushima Daiichi reactor incident. While focus DRC Trials is advance for use austere and inhospitable environments, objectives are progress areas supervised autonomy mobile manipulation everyday robotics. NASA's Johnson Space Center led a team comprised numerous partners develop Valkyrie, first bipedal humanoid robot. Valkyrie 44 degree‐of‐freedom, series elastic actuator‐based robot that draws upon over 18 years design heritage. Valkyrie's application intent aimed not only responding events like Fukushima, but also advancing human spaceflight endeavors extraterrestrial planetary settings. This paper presents brief system overview, detailing mechatronic subsystems, followed summarization inverse kinematics‐based walking algorithm employed Trials. Next, software control architectures highlighted along with description operator interface tools. Finally, some closing remarks given about competition, vision future work provided.

abstract: Abstract In recent years, surface codes have become a leading method for quantum error correction in theoretical large-scale computational and communications architecture designs. Our lattice surgery comprises splitting merging surfaces, enables us perform universal computation (including magic state injection) while removing need braided logic strictly design, hence reduces overall resources operations. title: Surface code quantum computing with error rates over 1%
abstract:
Large-scale quantum computation will only be achieved if experimentally
implementable error correction procedures are devised that can tolerate
experimentally achievable rates. We describe a correction
procedure requires 2-D square lattice of qubits interact
with their nearest neighbors, yet tolerate gate rates over
1%. The precise maximum tolerable rate depends on the model, and we
calculate values in range 1.1--1.4% for various physically reasonable
models. Even lowest value represents highest threshold rate
calculated to date geometrically constrained setting, 50%
improvement over previous record.
authors: David S. Wang, Austin G. Fowler, Lloyd C. L. Hollenberg
affiliations: The University of Melbourne; The University of Melbourne; The University of Melbourne

title: Microwaves in Quantum Computing
abstract: Quantum information processing systems rely on a broad range of microwave technologies and have spurred development devices methods in new operating regimes. Here we review the use signals quantum computing, with specific reference to three leading computing platforms: trapped atomic ion qubits, spin qubits semiconductors, superconducting qubits. We highlight some key results progress achieved through systems, discuss how applications pushed frontiers technology areas. also describe open engineering challenges for construction large-scale, fault-tolerant computers.

title: Strawberry Fields: A Software Platform for Photonic Quantum Computing
abstract: We introduce Strawberry Fields, an open-source quantum programming architecture for light-based computers, and detail its key features. Built in Python, Fields is a full-stack library design, simulation, optimization, machine learning of continuous-variable circuits. The platform consists three main components: (i) API based on easy-to-use language named Blackbird; (ii) suite virtual computer backends, built NumPy TensorFlow, each targeting specialized uses; (iii) engine which can compile Blackbird programs various including the built-in simulators, - near future photonic information processors. also contains examples several paradigmatic algorithms, teleportation, (Gaussian) boson sampling, instantaneous polynomial, Hamiltonian variational circuit optimization.

abstract:

two-qubit operations for this system discuss prospects fabrication
and scale up.

title: Quantum chaos border for quantum computing
abstract: We study a generic model of quantum computer, composed many qubits coupled by short-range interaction. Above critical interqubit coupling strength, chaos sets in, leading to ergodicity the computer eigenstates. In this regime noninteracting qubit structure disappears, eigenstates become complex, and operability is destroyed. Despite fact that spacing between multiqubit states drops exponentially with number n, we show border decreases only linearly n. This opens broad parameter region where efficient operation remains possible.

title: Quantum Computing with Rotation-Symmetric Bosonic Codes
abstract:
Bosonic rotation codes, introduced here, are a broad class of bosonic
error-correcting codes based on phase-space symmetry. We present a
universal quantum computing scheme applicable to subset this
class--number-phase codes--which includes the well-known cat and binomial
codes, among many others. The entangling gate in our is code-agnostic
and can be used interface different rotation-symmetric encodings. In
addition universal set operations, we propose teleportation-based
error correction that allows recoveries tracked entirely in
software. Focusing binomial as examples, compute average

for fault-tolerant, concatenation of

abstract: We report an experimental realization of one-way quantum computing on a two-photon four-qubit cluster state. abstract: Abstract Among the various approaches to quantum computing, all-optical architectures are especially promising due robustness and mobility of single photons. Here we propose two-qubit gate, where qubits encoded in surface plasmons graphene nanostructures, that exploits graphene's strong third-order nonlinearity long plasmon lifetimes enable single-photon-level interactions. title: Experimental Quantum Computing to Solve Systems of Linear Equations
abstract: Solving linear systems of equations is ubiquitous in all areas science and engineering. With rapidly growing data sets, such a task can be intractable for classical computers, as the best known algorithms require time proportional to number variables N. A recently proposed quantum algorithm shows that computers could solve scale order log(N), giving an exponential speedup over computers. Here we realize simplest instance this algorithm, solving 2×2 various input vectors on computer. We use four bits controlled logic gates implement every subroutine required, demonstrating working principle algorithm.

title: Shor's discrete logarithm quantum algorithm for elliptic curves
abstract: We show in some detail how to implement Shor's efficient quantum algorithm for discrete logarithms the particular case of elliptic curve groups. It turns out that this problem a smaller computer can solve problems further beyond current computing than integer factorisation. A 160 bit cryptographic key could be broken on using around 1000 qubits while factoring security-wise equivalent 1024 RSA modulus would require about 2000 qubits. In paper we only consider curves over GF(p) and not yet equally important ones GF(2^n) or other finite fields. The main technical difficulty is Euclid's gcd compute multiplicative inverses modulo p. As runtime depends input, one encountered ``quantum halting problem''.

title: Polynomial-time quantum algorithm for the simulation of chemical dynamics
abstract: The computational cost of exact methods for quantum simulation using classical computers grows exponentially with system size. As a consequence, these techniques can be applied only to small systems. By contrast, we demonstrate that could exactly simulate chemical reactions in polynomial time. Our algorithm uses the split-operator approach and explicitly simulates all electron-nuclear interelectronic interactions quadratic Surprisingly, this treatment is not more accurate than Born–Oppenheimer approximation but faster efficient as well, about four atoms. This case even though entire electronic wave function propagated on grid appropriately short time steps. Although preparation measurement arbitrary states computer inefficient, here how prepare interest efficiently. We also show efficiently obtain chemically relevant observables, such state-to-state transition probabilities thermal reaction rates. Quantum outperform current 100 qubits.

title: Advances in methods and algorithms in a modern quantum chemistry program package
abstract: Advances in theory and algorithms for electronic structure calculations must be incorporated into program packages to enable them become routinely used by the broader chemical community. This work reviews advances made over past five years or so that constitute major improvements contained a new release of Q-Chem quantum chemistry package, together with illustrative timings applications. Specific developments discussed include fast methods density functional calculations, linear scaling evaluation energies, NMR shifts electric properties, auxiliary basis function correlated energies gradients, equation-of-motion coupled cluster ground excited states, geminal wavefunctions, embedding techniques exploring potential energy surfaces.

title: Learning the quantum algorithm for state overlap
abstract: Short-depth algorithms are crucial for reducing computational error on near-term quantum computers, which decoherence and gate infidelity remain important issues. Here we present a machine-learning approach discovering such algorithms. We apply our method to ubiquitous primitive: computing the overlap $\\mathrm{Tr}(\\rho \\sigma )$ between two states ρ σ. The standard algorithm this task, known as Swap Test, is used in many applications support vector machines, and, when specialized = σ, quantifies Renyi entanglement. Here, find that have shorter depths than including one has constant depth (independent of problem size). Furthermore, hardware-specific connectivity sets by Rigetti's IBM's computers demonstrate derive significantly reduce error—compared Test—on these computers.

title: A Quantum Adiabatic Evolution Algorithm Applied to Random Instances of an NP-Complete Problem
abstract: A quantum system will stay near its instantaneous ground state if the Hamiltonian that governs evolution varies slowly enough. This adiabatic behavior is basis of a new class algorithms for computing. We tested one such algorithm by applying it to randomly generated hard instances an NP-complete problem. For small examples we could simulate, worked well, providing evidence computers (if large ones can be built) may able outperform ordinary on sets problems.

abstract: The clock synchronization problem is to determine the time difference Delta between two spatially separated clocks. For natural notion well-clusterable datasets, becomes \frac{η^{2.5}}{δ^3} k^{2.5} \frac{η^2}{δ^3} number features $d$, polynomial rank $k$, maximum square norm $η$ error $δ$. Both times are only polylogarithmic datapoints $N$. Our provides substantial savings compared $k$-means runs $O(kdN)$ particularly case large datasets.

title: Experimental realization of quantum algorithm for solving linear systems of equations
abstract:
Quantum computers have the potential of solving certain problems
exponentially faster than classical computers. Recently, Harrow, Hassidim and
Lloyd proposed a quantum algorithm for linear systems equations:
given an $N\times{N}$ matrix $A$ and vector $\vec b$, find $\vec
x$ that satisfies $A\vec x = \vec b$. It has been shown using the
algorithm one could obtain solution encoded in state $|x$ using
$O(\log{N})$ operations, while algorithms require at least
O(N) steps. If is not interested $\vec{x}$ itself but
certain statistical feature ${x}|M|x$ ($M$ some quantum
mechanical operator), will be able to achieve exponential
speedup over best as $N$ grows. Here we report a
proof-of-concept experimental demonstration a
4-qubit nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) information processor. For all
the three sets experiments with different choices the
solutions 96% fidelity. This experiment first implementation of
the algorithm. Because common problem nearly all
fields science engineering, also discuss implication our
results on practical
linear systems.

title: Quantum algorithm for association rules mining
abstract:
Association rules mining (ARM) is one of the most important problems in
knowledge discovery and data mining. Given a transaction database that has a
large number transactions items, task ARM to acquire
consumption habits customers by discovering relationships between
itemsets (sets items). In this paper, we address in quantum settings
and propose algorithm for key part ARM, finding out frequent
itemsets from candidate itemsets acquiring their supports.
Specifically, case which there are $M_f^{(k)}$ frequent $k$-itemsets
in $M_c^{(k)}$ $k$-itemsets ($M_f^{(k)} \leq M_c^{(k)}$), our
algorithm can efficiently mine these estimate their
supports using parallel amplitude estimation amplification
with complexity $\mathcal{O}(\frac{k\sqrt{M_c^{(k)}M_f^{(k)}}}{\epsilon})$,
where $\epsilon$ error estimating supports. Compared with the
classical counterpart, classical sampling-based algorithm, whose is
$\mathcal{O}(\frac{kM_c^{(k)}}{\epsilon^2})$, our algorithm
quadratically improves dependence on both the
best when $M_f^{(k)}\ll M_c^{(k)}$ alone worst
case $M_f^{(k)}\approx M_c^{(k)}$.

abstract: We present and experimentally realize a quantum algorithm for efficiently solving the following problem: given an $N\times N$ matrix $\mathcal{M}$, $N$-dimensional vector $\textbf{\emph{b}}$, initial $\textbf{\emph{x}}(0)$, obtain target $\textbf{\emph{x}}(t)$ as function of time $t$ according to constraint $d\textbf{\emph{x}}(t)/dt=\mathcal{M}\textbf{\emph{x}}(t)+\textbf{\emph{b}}$. show that our exhibits exponential speedup over its classical counterpart in certain circumstances. In addition, we demonstrate $4\times4$ linear differential equation using 4-qubit nuclear magnetic resonance information processor. Our provides key technique many important problems which rely on solutions equations.

title: Holographic quantum algorithms for simulating correlated spin systems
abstract:
We present a suite of "holographic" quantum algorithms for efficient
ground-state preparation and dynamical evolution correlated spin-systems,
which require far-fewer qubits than the number spins being simulated. The
algorithms exploit equivalence between matrix-product states (MPS) and
quantum channels, along with partial measurement qubit re-use, in order to
simulate $D$-dimensional spin system using only ($D$-1)-dimensional subset
of an ancillary register whose size scales
logarithmically amount entanglement simulated state.
Ground can either be directly prepared from known MPS representation,
or obtained via holographic variational eigensolver (holoVQE).
Dynamics under local Hamiltonians time $t$ also simulated
with additional (multiplicative) ${\rm poly}(t)$ overhead qubit
resources. These techniques open door to efficient simulation of
MPS exponentially large bond-dimension, including ground-states 2D and
3D systems, or thermalizing dynamics rapid growth. As a
demonstration potential resource savings, we implement holoVQE
simulation antiferromagnetic Heisenberg chain on trapped-ion quantum
computer, achieving within $10(3)\%$ exact ground-state energy an
infinite pair qubits.

title: Quantum-optimal-control-inspired ansatz for variational quantum algorithms
abstract:

title: Quantum algorithm for support matrix machines
abstract: We propose a quantum algorithm for support matrix machines (SMMs) that efficiently addresses an image classification problem by introducing least-squares reformulation. This consists of two core subroutines: inversion (Harrow-Hassidim-Lloyd, HHL) and singular value thresholding (QSVT) algorithm. The algorithms can be implemented on universal computer with complexity $O\left[log\left(npq\right)\right]$ $O\left[log\left(pq\right)\right]$, respectively, where $n$ is the number training data $pq$ size feature space. By iterating algorithms, we find parameters SMM classfication model. Our analysis shows both HHL QSVT achieve exponential increase speed over their classical counterparts.

title: From Pulses to Circuits and Back Again: A Quantum Optimal Control Perspective on Variational Quantum Algorithms
abstract: The last decade has witnessed remarkable progress in the development of quantum technologies. Although fault-tolerant devices likely remain years away, noisy intermediate-scale today may be leveraged for other purposes. Leading candidates are variational algorithms (VQAs), which have been developed applications including chemistry, optimization, and machine learning, but whose implementations on yet to demonstrate improvements over classical capabilities. In this Perspective, we propose a variety ways that performance VQAs could informed by optimal control theory. A major theme throughout is need sufficient resources VQA implementations; discuss different can manifest, outline open questions, look future.

title: Quantum Algorithms for Element Distinctness
abstract:
We present several applications of quantum amplitude amplification to finding
claws and collisions in ordered or unordered functions. Our algorithms
generalize those Brassard, Hoyer, Tapp, imply an O(N^{3/4} log N)
quantum upper bound for the element distinctness problem comparison
complexity model (contrasting with Theta(N N) classical complexity). We
also prove a lower Omega(N^{1/2}) comparisons this and
derive bounds number related problems.

title: A quantum algorithm to train neural networks using low-depth circuits
abstract: Can near-term gate model based quantum processors offer advantage for practical applications in the pre-fault tolerance noise regime? A class of algorithms which have shown some promise this regard are so-called classical-quantum hybrid variational algorithms. Here we develop a low-depth algorithm to generative neural networks using circuits. We introduce method employs approximate optimization as subroutine order produce then sample low-energy distributions Ising Hamiltonians. these states train and demonstrate training convergence numerically simulated noisy circuits with depolarizing errors rates up $4\%$.

title: Adiabatic quantum algorithms as quantum phase transitions: First versus second order
abstract:
In the continuum limit (large number of qubits), adiabatic quantum algorithms
display a remarkable similarity to sweeps through phase transitions. We
find that transitions second or higher order are advantageous in comparison
to those first order. With this insight, we propose novel adiabatic
quantum algorithm for solution 3-satisfiability (3-SAT) problems (exact
cover), which is significantly faster than previous proposals according to
numerical simulations (up 20 qubits). These findings suggest algorithms can solve NP-complete such as 3-SAT much faster
than Grover search routine (yielding quadratic enhancement), possibly
even with an exponential speed-up. PACS: 03.67.-a, 03.67.Lx, 73.43.Nq,
64.70.-p.
authors: Ralf Schützhold, Gernot Schaller
affiliations: Technische Universität Dresden; Technische Universität Dresden

title: Quantum algorithms via linear algebra: a primer
abstract: This introduction to quantum algorithms is concise but comprehensive, covering many key algorithms. It mathematically rigorous requires minimal background and assumes no knowledge of theory or mechanics. The book explains computation in terms elementary linear algebra; it the reader will have some familiarity with vectors, matrices, their basic properties, offers a review all relevant material from algebra. After explaining development operations computations based on algebra, presents major algorithms, seminal by Deutsch, Jozsa, Simon through Shor's Grover's recent walks. covers gates, computational complexity, graph theory. title: Feasible Entanglement Purification for Quantum Communication
abstract:
The distribution of entangled states between distant locations will be
essential for the future large scale realization quantum communication
schemes such as cryptography and teleportation. Because the
unavoidable noise in communication channel, entanglement
between two particles is more degraded further they propagate.
Entanglement purification thus essential to distill highly states
from less ones. Existing general protocols are based on
the controlled-NOT (CNOT) or similar logic operations, which
are very difficult implement experimentally. Present realizations CNOT
gates much too imperfect be useful long-distance quantum
communication. Here we present a feasible scheme entanglement
purification mixed states, which does not require any CNOT
operations, but only simple linear optical elements. Since perfection of
such elements high, local operations necessary can
be performed with required precision. Our procedure within reach of
current technology should significantly simplify implementation of
long-distance communication.

title: Strategies for enhancing quantum entanglement by local photon subtraction
abstract:
Subtracting photons from a two-mode squeezed state is well-known method to
increase entanglement. We analyse different strategies of local photon
subtraction in terms entanglement gain and
success probability. develop general framework that incorporates
imperfections and losses all stages the process: before, during, and
after subtraction. By combining three effects into single efficiency

strategies using photon-number-resolving threshold detectors. compare

taking each only input can the

initial parameter. Finally, discuss implications our
results distillation continuous-variable quantum entanglement.

abstract: Entanglement concentration is of most importance in long distance quantum communication and computation. It to distill maximally entangled states from pure partially based on the local operation classical communication. In this review, we will mainly describe two kinds entanglement protocols. title: Quantum entanglement as an interpretation of bosonic character in composite two-particle systems
abstract:
We consider a composite particle formed by two fermions or bosons. We
discover that behavior is deeply related to the quantum entanglement
between constituent particles. By analyzing properties of creation and
annihilation operators, we show bosonic character emerges if the
constituent particles become strongly entangled. Such connection is
demonstrated explicitly in class two-particle wave functions.

abstract: We use spatial light modulators to observe the quantum entanglement of down-converted photon pairs. Acting as diffractive optical elements within one beams, they can be reconfigured in real time set profile measured mode. Such configurations are highly applicable measurement orbital angular momentum states or other modes, such those associated with imaging.

abstract: We develop a general theory of the relation between quantum phase transitions (QPTs) characterized by nonanalyticities in energy and bipartite entanglement. derive functional matrix elements two-particle reduced density matrices eigenvalues two-body Hamiltonians d-level systems. The ground state eigenvalue its derivatives, whose nonanalyticity characterizes QPT, are directly tied to entanglement measures. show that first-order QPTs signaled themselves second-order first derivative elements. title: Quantum entanglement on photonic chips: a review
abstract: Entanglement is one of the most vital properties quantum mechanical systems, and it forms backbone information technologies. Taking advantage nano/microfabrication particularly complementary metal-oxide-semiconductor manufacturing technologies, photonic integrated circuits (PICs) have emerged as a versatile platform for generation, manipulation, measurement entangled states. We summarize recent progress entanglement on PICs, starting from generation nonentangled biphoton states, to states multiple photons, dimensions, degrees freedom, well their applications processing.

abstract: Based on quantum entanglement, an all-or-nothing oblivious transfer (OT) protocol is proposed and proven to be secure. title: Quantum simulation
abstract:
Simulating quantum mechanics is known to be a difficult computational
problem, especially when dealing with large systems. However, this difficulty
may overcome by using some controllable system study another less
controllable or accessible system, i.e., simulation. Quantum
simulation promises have applications in the of many problems in,
e.g., condensed-matter physics, high-energy atomic quantum
chemistry and cosmology. Quantum simulation could implemented quantum
computers, but also simpler, analog devices that would require less
control, therefore, easier construct. A number quantum
systems such as neutral atoms, ions, polar molecules, electrons in
semiconductors, superconducting circuits, nuclear spins photons been
proposed simulators. This review outlines main theoretical and
experimental aspects emphasizes the
challenges fast-growing field.

Ultracold atoms optical lattices represent an ideal platform for simulations many-body problems. Within this setting, gas microscopes enable single atom observation and manipulation large samples. atom–based simulators have already been used to probe magnetism, realize detect topological matter, study systems with controlled long-range interactions. abstract:

title: Quantum Simulation of Generic Many-Body Open System Dynamics Using Classical Noise
abstract: We introduce a scheme for the quantum simulation of many-body decoherence based on unitary evolution stochastic Hamiltonian. Our proposal readily implemented in variety platforms including optical lattices, superconducting circuits, and trapped ions.

title: Digital Quantum Simulation of the Schwinger Model and Symmetry Protection with Trapped Ions
abstract: Tracking the dynamics of physical systems in real time is a prime application quantum computers. Using trapped-ion system with up to six qubits, we simulate real-time lattice gauge theory 1+1 dimensions, i.e., Schwinger model, and demonstrate nonperturbative effects such as pair creation for times much longer than previously accessible. We study gate requirement two formulations model using Suzuki-Trotter product formula, well trade-off between errors from ordering Hamiltonian terms, Trotter step size, experimental imperfections. To mitigate errors, recent symmetry-protection protocol suppressing coherent symmetry-inspired postselection scheme are applied. This work demonstrates integrated theoretical, algorithmic, approach that essential efficient simulation theories other complex systems.

title: Quantum Simulation of Helium Hydride Cation in a Solid-State Spin Register
abstract: Ab initio computation of molecular properties is one the most promising applications quantum computing. While this problem widely believed to be intractable for classical computers, efficient algorithms exist which have potential vastly accelerate research throughput in fields ranging from material science drug discovery. Using a solid-state register realized nitrogen-vacancy (NV) defect diamond, we compute bond dissociation curve minimal basis helium hydride cation, HeH(+). Moreover, report an energy uncertainty (given our model basis) order 10(-14) hartree, 10 orders magnitude below desired chemical precision. As NV centers diamond provide robust and straightforward platform information processing, work provides important step toward fully scalable implementation chemistry simulator.

title: Quantum Simulations of Physics Problems
abstract: If a large Quantum Computer (QC) existed today, what type of physical problems could we efficiently simulate on it that not classical Turing machine? In this paper argue QC solve some relevant "questions" more efficiently. The existence one-to-one mappings between different algebras observables or Hilbert spaces allow us to represent and imitate any system by other one (e.g. bosonic spin-1/2 system). We explain how these can be performed, show quantum networks useful for the efficient evaluation properties, such as correlation functions energy spectra.

title: Optimal Surface-Electrode Trap Lattices for Quantum Simulation with Trapped Ions
abstract: Trapped ions offer long internal state (spin) coherence times and strong interparticle interactions mediated by the Coulomb force. title: Revealing anyonic features in a toric code quantum simulation
abstract: Anyons are quasiparticles in two-dimensional systems that show statistical properties very distinct from those of bosons or fermions. While their isolated observation has not yet been achieved, here we perform a quantum simulation anyons on the toric code model. By encoding model multi-partite entangled state polarized photons, able to demonstrate various manipulations anyonic states and, particular, characteristic fractional statistics.

title: Observing the Quantum Mpemba Effect in Quantum Simulations
abstract: The nonequilibrium physics of many-body quantum systems harbors various unconventional phenomena. In this Letter, we experimentally investigate one the most puzzling these phenomena-the Mpemba effect, where a tilted ferromagnet restores its symmetry more rapidly when it is farther from symmetric state compared to closer. title: Quantum Simulation of Collective Proton Tunneling in Hexagonal Ice Crystals
abstract: The effect of proton tunneling on many-body correlated transfer in hexagonal ice is investigated by quantum simulation. Classical single-particle hopping along individual hydrogen bonds leads to charge defects at high temperature, whereas six protons ringlike topologies can move concertedly as a delocalized quasiparticle via collective low thus preventing the creation high-energy topological defects. Our findings rationalize hydrogen-bonded networks and suggest that this phenomenon might be more widespread than previously thought.

title: Quantum simulation and phase diagram of the transverse-field Ising model with three atomic spins
abstract: We perform a quantum simulation of the Ising model with transverse field using collection three trapped atomic ion spins. By adiabatically manipulating Hamiltonian, we directly probe ground state for wide range fields and form couplings, leading to phase diagram magnetic order in this microscopic system. The technique is scalable much larger numbers spins, where transitions approaching thermodynamic limit can be studied cases theory becomes intractable.

title: Genetic Algorithms for Digital Quantum Simulations
abstract: We propose genetic algorithms, which are robust optimization techniques inspired by natural selection, to enhance the versatility of digital quantum simulations. In this sense, we show that algorithms can be employed increase fidelity and optimize resource requirements simulation protocols while adapting naturally experimental constraints. Furthermore, method allows us reduce not only errors but also in gates. Indeed, adding ancillary qubits, design a modular gate made out imperfect gates, whose is larger than any constituent Finally, prove proposed gates resilient against different errors.

title: Real-time chiral dynamics from a digital quantum simulation
abstract: The chiral magnetic effect in a strong field can be described using the anomaly (1+1)-dimensional massive Schwinger model with time-dependent θ term. We perform digital quantum simulation of at finite angle and vanishing gauge coupling an IBM-Q simulator, observe corresponding vector current induced system relativistic fermions by global quench—a sudden change chemical potential or angle. One of results is that Elliptic Curve (ECC) fits here perfectly, providing level security RSA does, but much shorter keys. this paper, we evaluate power consumption induced by various approaches including calculation transmission costs. Our clearly show hardware accelerators significantly reduce energy prolong lifetime sensor nodes. solution 163 bit ECC needs only 1.02 mm² cell area 0.25 µm technology 12.8 µWs per point multiplication. title: Research on Certificateless Public Key Cryptography
abstract: PDF HTML阅读 XML下载 导出引用 引用提醒 无证书公钥密码体制研究 DOI: 10.3724/SP.J.1001.2011.04007 作者: 作者单位: 作者简介: 通讯作者: 中图分类号: 基金项目: 国家自然科学基金(60673070); 江苏省自然科学基金(BK2006217); 南京工业大学青年教师学术基金(39704023) Research on Certificateless Public Key Cryptography Author: Affiliation: Fund Project: 摘要 | 图/表 访问统计 参考文献 相似文献 引证文献 资源附件 文章评论 摘要:无证书公钥密码体制(certificateless public key cryptography,简称CL-PKC)是在基于身份的公钥密码体制(identity-based cryptography,简称ID-PKC)的基础上提出来的一种新型公钥密码体制,没有密钥托管问题、不需要使用公钥证书,使得无证书公钥密码体制从其概念提出的初始就受到了学术界和工业界的极大关注.从2003 年至今,它一直是密码学和信息安全领域非常活跃的研究热点.其理论和技术在不断地丰富和发展.到目前为止,已经积累了大量的研究成果.将对这些成果进行较为系统的整理、分析、比较和简要的评述,并探讨该领域研究尚存在的不足及值得进一步研究的问题. Abstract:Certificateless cryptography (CL-PKC for short) is a new type of cryptography, which developed the foundation identity based (ID-PKC short). CL-PKC elimilates escrow problem and need certificate. These two advantages are what makes it attractive to research community industrial world form beginning its birth. It has been very active hot topic in field cryptology information security. In about seven years, study advanced step by step, making theories techniques more enriching. This paper revisits, analyzes, compares, briefly reviews some main results. Furthermore, this discusses existing problems that deserve further investigation.
authors: Futai Zhang, Yinxia Sun, Lei Zhang, Manman Geng, Sujuan Li
affiliations: Nanjing Normal University, Nanjing Tech University; Nanjing Normal University; Universitat Rovira i Virgili; Nanjing Normal University; Nanjing Normal University, Nanjing Tech University

title: A Blockchain-Based Authentication and Security Mechanism for IoT
abstract: The existing identity authentication of IoT devices mostly depends on an intermediary institution, i.e., a CA server, which suffers from the single-point-failure attack. Even worse, critical data authenticated can be tampered by inner attacks without being identified. To address these issues, we utilize blockchain technology, serves as secure tamper-proof distributed ledger to devices. In proposed method, assign unique ID for each individual device and record them into blockchain, so that they authenticate other central authority. We also design protection mechanism hashing significant (i.e. firmware) where any state changes detected immediately. Finally, implement prototype based open source platform Hyperledger Fabric verify system.

title: Leveraging zero knowledge proofs for blockchain-based identity sharing: A survey of advancements, challenges and opportunities
abstract: Identity sharing systems, regardless of their architectural models, share common vulnerabilities. These systems compel users to divulge personal information and furnish proof identity for accessing services, leaving them susceptible data breaches that can culminate in theft jeopardize online security. While blockchain technology offers a potential remedy, delivering enhanced security, immutability, traceability, it simultaneously raises pertinent concerns surrounding privacy transparency. title: PipeZK: Accelerating Zero-Knowledge Proof with a Pipelined Architecture
abstract: Zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) is a promising cryptographic protocol for both computation integrity and privacy. It can be used in many privacy-preserving applications including verifiable cloud outsourcing blockchains. The major obstacle of using ZKP practice its time-consuming step generation, which consists large-size polynomial computations multi-scalar multiplications on elliptic curves. To efficiently practically support real-world applications, we propose PipeZK, pipelined accelerator with two subsystems to handle the aforementioned intensive compute tasks, respectively. first subsystem uses novel dataflow decompose large kernels into smaller ones that execute bandwidth-efficient hardware modules, optimized off-chip memory accesses on-chip resources. second adopts lightweight dynamic work dispatch mechanism share heavy processing units, minimized resource underutilization load imbalance. When evaluated 28 nm, PipeZK achieve 10x speedup standard benchmarks, 5x widely-used cryptocurrency application, Zcash.

Because protocols grow linearly integer length, proofs will eventually be longer those produced by quantum-safe succinct systems general circuits (e.g. Ligero, Aurora, etc.). abstract: The emerging blockchain technology, combined with the smart contract paradigm, is expected to transform traditional applications decentralization. When technology applied decentralize applications, validators may need take in sensitive off-chain data execute a contract. On one hand, decentralized (DApps) require authentic off-chain input correctly given business procedure. other users are reluctant expose their privacy on blockchain. For example, for medical insurance DApp that takes as personal health data, it critical guarantee authenticity and of sent contract, such can be verified by without leaking information. However, no satisfactory solution has been proposed attain at same time. In this work, we first present highly efficient authenticated zero knowledge proof protocol called zk-DASNARK extending classical zk-SNARK scheme authentication. Based zk-DASNARK, design zk-AuthFeed, zero-knowledge feed achieve both blockchain-based DApps. Following strategy "compute verify on-chain", zk-AuthFeed significantly reduce computation cost validators. We fully implement prototype conduct comprehensive experiments DApp. consider 4 typical models premium/reimbursement experiments. title: On How Zero-Knowledge Proof Blockchain Mixers Improve, and Worsen User Privacy
abstract: Zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) mixers are one of the most widely-used blockchain privacy solutions, operating on top smart contract-enabled blockchains. We find that ZKP tightly intertwined with growing number Decentralized Finance (DeFi) attacks and Blockchain Extractable Value (BEV) extractions. Through coin flow tracing, we discover 205 attackers 2, 595 BEV extractors leverage as their source funds, while depositing a total attack revenue 412.87M USD. Moreover, US OFAC sanctions against largest mixer, Tornado.Cash, have reduced mixer's daily deposits by more than .

title: Adaptive Group-Based Zero Knowledge Proof-Authentication Protocol in Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks
abstract:
Vehicular Ad Hoc Networks (VANETs) are a particular subclass of mobile ad hoc
networks that raise number security challenges, notably from the way users
authenticate network. Authentication technologies based on existing
security policies and access control rules in such networks assume full trust
on Roadside Unit (RSU) authentication servers. The disclosure of
authentication parameters enables user's trace-ability over network.
VANETs' trusted entities (e.g. RSU) can utilize information to track a
user traveling behavior, violating user privacy anonymity. In this paper,
we proposed novel, light-weight, Adaptive Group-based Zero Knowledge
Proof-Authentication Protocol (AGZKP-AP) for VANETs. proposed
authentication protocol is capable offering various levels users' privacy
settings type services available networks. Our scheme
is Zero-Knowledge-Proof (ZKP) crypto approach with support of
trade-off options. Users have option make critical decisions the
level amount resources usage they prefer as short
system response time versus private disclosures.
Furthermore, AGZKP-AP incorporated distributed privilege and
revoking mechanism render law enforcement in
case traffic violation.

title: Securing Smart Manufacturing by Integrating Anomaly Detection With Zero-Knowledge Proofs
abstract: In the rapidly advancing domain of smart manufacturing, securing data integrity and preventing unauthorized access are critical challenges. title: Computational soundness of symbolic zero-knowledge proofs*
abstract: The abstraction of cryptographic operations by term algebras, called Dolev–Yao models, is essential in almost all tool-supported methods for proving security protocols. Recently significant progress was made that models offering the core such as encrypt ion and digital signatures can be sound with respect to actual realizations definitions. Recent work, however, has started extend more sophisticated unique features. Zero-knowledge proofs arguably constitute most amazing extension. In this paper, we first identify which additional properties a (non-interactive) zero-knowledge proof needs fulfill order serve computationally implementation symbolic (Dolev–Yao style) proofs; leads novel definition symbolically-sound system. We prove even presence arbitrary active adversaries, systems implementations proofs. This yields computational soundness result against fully adversaries go beyond operations.

title: BANZKP: a Secure Authentication Scheme Using Zero Knowledge Proof for\n WBANs
abstract:
-Wireless body area network(WBAN) has shown great potential in improving
healthcare quality not only for patients but also medical staff. However,
security and privacy are still an important issue WBANs especially in
multi-hop architectures. In this paper, we propose present the design and
the evaluation of a secure lightweight energy efficient authentication
scheme BANZKP based on cryptographic protocol, Zero Knowledge
Proof (ZKP) commitment scheme. ZKP is used to confirm identify the
sensor nodes, with small computational requirement, which favorable body
sensors given their limited resources, while scheme to
deal replay attacks hence injection by committing a
message revealing key later. Our reduces memory requirement
by 56.13 % compared TinyZKP [13], comparable alternative so far Body
Area Networks, uses 10 less energy.

title: Coronavirus Genomics and Bioinformatics Analysis
abstract: The drastic increase in the number of coronaviruses discovered and coronavirus genomes being sequenced have given us an unprecedented opportunity to perform genomics bioinformatics analysis on this family viruses. Coronaviruses possess largest (26.4 31.7 kb) among all known RNA viruses, with G + C contents varying from 32% 43%. Variable numbers small ORFs are present between various conserved genes (ORF1ab, spike, envelope, membrane nucleocapsid) downstream nucleocapsid gene different lineages. Phylogenetically, three genera, Alphacoronavirus, Betacoronavirus Gammacoronavirus, consisting subgroups A, B, D, exist. A fourth genus, Deltacoronavirus, which includes bulbul HKU11, thrush HKU12 munia HKU13, is emerging. Molecular clock using loci revealed that time most recent common ancestor human/civet SARS related be 1999-2002, estimated substitution rate 4´10-4 2´10-2 substitutions per site year. Recombination was notable strains murine hepatitis virus (MHV), infectious bronchitis virus, MHV bovine coronavirus, feline (FCoV) type I canine generating FCoV II, genotypes human HKU1 (HCoV-HKU1). abstract: Since 2009 the EMBL-EBI Job Dispatcher framework has provided free access to a range of mainstream sequence analysis applications. Through these users can databases ENA, UniProt Ensembl Genomes, utilising uniform web interface or systematically through Web Services interfaces (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/Tools/webservices/) using common programming languages, obtain enriched results with novel visualisations. Integration EBI Search (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/ebisearch/) dbfetch retrieval service (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/Tools/dbfetch/) further expands usefulness framework. abstract: Abstract MicroRNAs (miRNAs) are small noncoding RNAs that regulate gene expression via recognition of cognate sequences and interference transcriptional, translational or epigenetic processes. title: Structural Bioinformatics and its Impact to Biomedical Science
abstract: During the last two decades, number of sequence-known proteins has increased rapidly. In contrast, corresponding increment for structure-known is much slower. The unbalanced situation critically limited our ability to understand molecular mechanism and conduct structure-based drug design by timely using updated information newly found sequences. Therefore, it highly desired develop an automated method fast deriving 3D (3-dimensional) structure a protein from its sequence. Under such circumstance, structural bioinformatics was emerging naturally as time required. this review, three main strategies developed in bioinformatics, i.e., pure energetic approach, heuristic homology modeling well their underlying principles, are briefly introduced. Meanwhile, series demonstrations presented show how been applied derive structures some functionally important proteins, helping action mechanisms stimulating course discovery. Also, limitation these approaches future challenges addressed.

title: A Review of Ensemble Methods in Bioinformatics
abstract: Ensemble learning is an intensively studied technique in machine and pattern recognition. Recent work computational biology has seen increasing use of ensemble methods due to their unique advantages dealing with small sample size, high-dimensionality, complex data structures. The aim this article two-fold. Firstly, it provide a review the most widely used application various bioinformatics problems, including main topics gene expression, mass spectrometry-based proteomics, gene-gene interaction identification from genome-wide association studies, prediction regulatory elements DNA protein sequences. Secondly, we try identify summarize future trends bioinformatics. Promising directions such as support vector machines, meta-ensembles, based feature selection are discussed. Keywords: learning, bioinformatics, microarray, interaction, prediction, meta ensemble, selection.

title: A brief history of bioinformatics
abstract: Abstract It is easy for today’s students and researchers to believe that modern bioinformatics emerged recently assist next-generation sequencing data analysis. However, the very beginnings of occurred more than 50 years ago, when desktop computers were still a hypothesis DNA could not yet be sequenced. The foundations laid in early 1960s with application computational methods protein sequence analysis (notably, de novo assembly, biological databases substitution models). Later on, also due parallel advances (i) molecular biology methods, which allowed easier manipulation DNA, as well its sequencing, (ii) computer science, saw rise increasingly miniaturized powerful computers, novel software better suited handle tasks. In 1990s through 2000s, major improvements technology, along reduced costs, gave an exponential increase data. arrival ‘Big Data’ has out new challenges terms mining management, calling expertise from science into field. Coupled ever-increasing amount tools, Big Data had (and continues have) profound implications on predictive power reproducibility results. To overcome this issue, universities are now fully integrating discipline curriculum students. title: Scaffold: A bioinformatic tool for validating MS/MS‐based proteomic studies
abstract: Abstract Over‐reporting of unreliable protein identifications has reduced the accuracy and reproducibility MS/MS‐based proteomic studies. In this work, we demonstrate analysis workflow used by a bioinformatic tool called Scaffold, which attempts to increase confidence in identification reports through use several statistical methods. addition, work describes an advanced grouping method Scaffold further reduce falsely reported identifications, particularly when using large or otherwise sequence redundant databases.

title: Structural Bioinformatics-Based Design of Selective, Irreversible Kinase Inhibitors
abstract: The active sites of 491 human protein kinase domains are highly conserved, which makes the design selective inhibitors a formidable challenge. We used structural bioinformatics approach to identify two selectivity filters, threonine and cysteine, at defined positions in site p90 ribosomal S6 (RSK). A fluoromethylketone inhibitor, designed exploit both potently selectively inactivated RSK1 RSK2 mammalian cells. Kinases with only one filter were resistant yet they became sensitized after genetic introduction second filter. Thus, amino acids that distinguish RSK from other kinases sufficient confer inhibitor sensitivity.
